question_title: What will be the value of "30-Year Fixed Rate Jumbo Mortgage Index" on 2026-03-12?
summary: The 30-Year Fixed Rate Jumbo Mortgage Index (OBMMIJUMBO30YF) shows strong mean-reverting behavior around ~6.18% in the current stable regime (since 2026-02-20). Daily changes have autocorrelation of -0.688, meaning moves quickly reverse. The underlying 30-year Treasury yield is very stable at 4.67-4.72%. An Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model with parameter uncertainty, calibrated on 19 daily observations, produces a distribution centered at ~6.18% with moderate uncertainty. The main risk is a macro regime shift from tariff/Fed/economic news that could move Treasury yields and thus mortgage rates, but the base case is continued oscillation around the current level.

factors.description: Last observed value is 6.214% on 2026-02-26, anchoring the forecast near current level
Strong mean reversion in daily changes (autocorrelation -0.688) produces tight stationary distribution around regime mean
Stable regime since 2026-02-20 with mean 6.1812 and std 0.055 - rates settled into lower range
30-year Treasury yields very stable at 4.67-4.72, providing stable floor for mortgage rates
Regime shift risk from tariffs, Fed, or economic data could move rates ±0.15-0.30 over 10 days

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

| Percentile | Value |
|------------|-------|
| P10 | 6.05% |
| P20 | 6.09% |
| P40 | 6.15% |
| P60 | 6.21% |
| P80 | 6.27% |
| P90 | 6.31% |

**Median estimate: ~6.18%**, essentially at the stable regime mean. The distribution is relatively tight (80% CI: 6.05–6.31%) because the series exhibits strong mean reversion — daily changes have -0.69 autocorrelation, meaning moves quickly reverse. The underlying 30-year Treasury yield is very stable at 4.67-4.72%, providing a stable anchor. The main tail risk is a macro shock (tariff escalation, Fed surprise, major economic data) that could shift Treasury yields and drag mortgage rates with them.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the value of "30-Year Fixed Rate Jumbo Mortgage Index" on 2026-03-12?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42389)
